proof fn phi_1_step_map_sound_no_existing_vmem_check(mappings: Map<nat, PTE>, inflights: Set<ThreadState>, vaddr: nat, pte: PTE)
    requires

        candidate_mapping_overlaps_existing_vmem(mappings, vaddr, pte),
    ensures

name: step_mapstart_unsound_allows_arbitrary_state
code:
proof fn phi_5_step_mapstart_unsound_allows_arbitrary_state(c: Constants, s1: State, s2: State, lbl: RLbl)
    requires
        lbl matches RLbl::MapStart { thread_id, vaddr, pte },
        step_Map_enabled(s1.thread_state.values(), s1.mappings, vaddr, pte),
        c.valid_thread(thread_id),
        s1.thread_state[thread_id] === ThreadState::Idle,
        !step_Map_sound(s1.mappings, s1.thread_state.values(), vaddr, pte),
        step_MapStart(c, s1, s2, lbl),
    ensures
        !s2.sound,
{
}
reason: When `step_Map_sound` fails, the system transitions to an unsound state — the entire state becomes untrusted rather than simply rejecting the operation, which is a permissive failure mode
